title: Epidemic Sound launches AI-powered ‘Studio’ - Music Ally
text:
AI is increasingly at the heart of Epidemic Sound’s business, although the company is continuing to take a careful approach so as not to be seen as undermining its roster of human musicians.
Its latest feature is called ‘Studio’ and is pitched as “the first AI-powered soundtracking tool that enables content creators to instantly match and place both human-made music and sound effects seamlessly within their video”.
So, this isn’t about making GenAI music for clients, but rather speeding up the process for filmmakers and video creators to mine Epidemic’s catalogue for suitable music – and then have it placed into a soundtrack to work on.
“We believe AI should empower, not replace, human creativity. Studio embodies that belief, giving creators more time and freedom to focus on their stories while continuing to help artists reach an audience and earn from their craft,” said CEO Oscar Höglund.
Stuart Dredge
Music Ally's Head of Insight
More by Stuart Dredge
